JSON Schema (atm.atomicMap) that every atomic_workbench/maps/**/map.spec.json must conform to
validator that walks atomic_workbench/maps/**/map.spec.json and validates each file against atom-map.schema.json
next selector / claim orchestrator: now treats unrelated untracked files as warning-only (ignoredUntrackedFiles) instead of hard-blocking claim (TASK-AAO-0011).

batch lifecycle, checkpoint delivery windows, and status: checkpoint can formally close scoped framework critical deliveries before the required single commit. TASK-AAO-0013: checkpoint failure returns structured category+requiredCommand (ATM_BATCH_CHECKPOINT_CLOSE_FAILED); partial completion uses ATM_BATCH_CHECKPOINT_PARTIAL_OK to prevent AI misreading partial-ok as batch-complete.
dogfood scorer. public_command_coverage reads packages/cli/src/commands/command-specs.ts as canonical public command catalog (TASK-AAO-0020); missing per-command help spec files counted as gaps.
canonical commandSpecs Object.freeze registry: source of truth for public_command_coverage scorer (TASK-AAO-0020).

task delivery intent classification (framework-delivery, mirror-sync-only, cross-repo-delivery) driven by imported machine fields (target_repo, closure_authority, planning_repo) and source-card status (TASK-AAO-0038).

tasks import/reconcile; import preserves nested evidence/rollback and target_repo/planning_repo/closure_authority; reconcile supports historical done task reconcile / reopen closure sync, evaluates deliverables gate on historical commits, syncs ledger done status without claim, aligns closure-packet attestation contract, and integrates planning-only warnings
shared command helpers, option parser parseOptions, output-json file writer redirection (TASK-AAO-0065), and structured CliError usage diagnostics envelopes

tasks scope add: formal scope amendment CLI; appends paths to active taskDirectionLock.allowedFiles via CLI, records scope-amendment transition event, returns ATM_SCOPE_AMENDMENT_APPLIED. Agents must not edit .atm/runtime/locks/** directly; all scope widening goes through this command (TASK-AAO-0010).
tasks command surface specification including scope add sub-command with --add csv option (TASK-AAO-0010).

task direction lock SSOT: writeTaskDirectionLock syncs governance lock top-level files with taskDirectionLock.allowedFiles; getCanonicalAllowedFilesForTask and diagnoseTaskDirectionLockAllowedFiles expose the canonical allowed-files set and report drift across governance lock files, embedded allowedFiles, and claim.files (TASK-AAO-0012). buildTaskSelfAllowPaths automatically merges the task's four canonical governance paths (.atm/history/tasks/<id>.json, .atm/history/evidence/<id>.json, .atm/history/evidence/<id>.closure-packet.json, .atm/history/task-events/<id>/**) into allowedFiles during claim without broadening to .atm/history/** (TASK-AAO-0058).

pre-commit hook runs diagnoseTaskDirectionLockAllowedFiles per active direction lock and emits ATM_DIRECTION_LOCK_ALLOWED_FILES_MISMATCH warning (non-blocking) with drift evidence when canonical allowedFiles diverges from governance lock files or claim.files (TASK-AAO-0012).
isPathAllowedByScope is the quickfix/batch scope path matcher; explicitly NOT the SSOT for task direction allowed files (TASK-AAO-0012).

next routing applies classifyTaskDelivery; planning_repo authority + foreign target_repo emits ATM_NEXT_TASK_MIRROR_SYNC_REQUIRED instead of normal delivery playbook; next --claim refuses mirror-sync-only tasks with ATM_NEXT_CLAIM_MIRROR_SYNC_REQUIRED (TASK-AAO-0038).

evidence CLI command: add, run, git-head-backfill, verify, diff, validators --list, missing; run command auto-spawns, captures stdout/stderr hashes, and adds evidence; --recent-run reuses cached results; validators --list returns per-task validator catalog with tier (focused/batch/milestone/release), expectedCommand, and evidenceState (pass/missing) sourced from framework requiredGates and task-declared validators (TASK-AAO-0015, TASK-AAO-0016); missing subcommand emits computeMissingValidatorReport — classifies each gate as pass/absent/failed-run/stale/diagnostic-only, scopes missingValidationPasses/blockingFindings to closure-required (focused + release + task-declared) validators, and surfaces batch-tier framework health gates as advisoryFindings that never block close; catalog entries carry closureRequired flag (TASK-AAO-0017, TASK-AAO-0017 follow-up); supports quoteForShell for cross-shell safe requiredCommand quoting and detectAutoLinkedValidator for auto-linking known validators during evidence add when no explicit validators are specified (TASK-AAO-0063).

evidence command surface specification including validators subcommand with --list --task flags, run with --recent-run, and missing subcommand with example (TASK-AAO-0015, TASK-AAO-0016, TASK-AAO-0017).

tasks close error envelopes enriched with TL;DR and structured missing-validator report: ATM_TASK_CLOSE_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED, ATM_TASK_CLOSE_FRAMEWORK_GATE_FAILED, ATM_TASK_CLOSE_CLOSURE_PACKET_INVALID all carry tldr, missingValidationPasses[{code,validator,category,summary,requiredCommand}], and blockingFindings[] via computeMissingValidatorReport — missingValidationPasses/blockingFindings now contain only closure-required validators so advisory framework health gates (doctor, framework-development, tasks-audit, git-head-evidence) do not falsely block close (TASK-AAO-0017, TASK-AAO-0017 follow-up).

close-gate scoped diff isolation: ATM_TASK_CLOSE_FRAMEWORK_DIFF_ACTIVE now triggers only when scopedCriticalChangedFiles (intersection of working-tree framework critical diffs with the task's declared scopePaths) is non-empty without a governed delivery window. evaluateFrameworkDeliveryWindow accepts --from-batch-checkpoint or --historical-delivery as the governance window and ignores unscoped (unrelated) critical changes. buildCloseScopedDiffIsolation emits atm.taskCloseScopedDiffIsolation.v1 diagnostic (declaredFiles, scopedCriticalChangedFiles, isolatedUnrelatedChanges, declaredButUnchanged, summary, advisoryNote) on both error details and close success evidence so agents can distinguish in-scope diffs from advisory unrelated changes and scope-overflow drift (TASK-AAO-0057).

batch checkpoint failure (ATM_BATCH_CHECKPOINT_CLOSE_FAILED) passes through tldr, missingValidationPasses, and blockingFindings from the inner tasks close failure via categorizeCheckpointCloseFailure; requiredCommand for missing-evidence now points to evidence missing subcommand (TASK-AAO-0017).

tasks deliver-and-close macro: bundles delivery commit (auto-stage scope-matching modified files + atm git commit) → historical close (tasks close --historical-delivery) → governance commit (stage task JSON, evidence, closure packet, transition event + atm git commit) into one command. Supports --delivery-commit to skip auto-staging and use a pre-existing commit. Batch guard: ATM_BATCH_CHECKPOINT_REQUIRED when task belongs to an active batch without --from-batch-checkpoint. Dry-run mode emits wouldAutoStage without modifying repo state (TASK-AAO-0056).

batch deliver-and-close macro: wraps tasks deliver-and-close with --from-batch-checkpoint for the active batch head, then advances the batch queue and optionally claims the next task (same post-close bookkeeping as batch checkpoint). Supports --delivery-commit, --message, --reason pass-through to the inner deliver-and-close. Partial-ok uses ATM_BATCH_DELIVER_AND_CLOSE_PARTIAL_OK; batch-complete uses ATM_BATCH_DELIVER_AND_CLOSE_OK (TASK-AAO-0056).
semantic versioning validation and comparison helpers, including isValidSemverVersionString and compareSemverVersions (hygiene patch)
lock and claim ledger reader helpers, list runtime task lock ids, parse and create claim records, and verify claim expiration status (TASK-AAO-0061)
Pure YAML/frontmatter/path parsing validators for task import pipeline
field-level envelope projection for --summary and --fields CLI flags
Pure parsing/normalization helpers for next.ts intent resolution: frontmatter parse, status/intent/scope normalizers, list splitters
